leadText: Chloroblast (Japanese: クロロブラスト Chloroblast) is a damage-dealing Grass-type move introduced during Generation VIII, in Pokémon Legends: Arceus. It is the signature move of Hisuian Electrode.

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Generation VIII [ edit | edit source ] Chloroblast inflicts damage, and the user takes damage equal to half of its maximum HP rounded up. It also lowers the user's action speed. Pokémon Legends: Arceus Category Special PP 5 Agile Strong Power 120 100 140 Accuracy 95% 95% 100% Action speed modifier Self +5 +2 +8 Target 0 +3 0 Additional effect Recoil 50% 33% 66% ? ? ? ? Damages the user by a percentage of its total HP Reduces the user's action speed Generation IX [ edit | edit source ] Chloroblast has a base power of 150. The user takes recoil damage equal to half of its maximum HP rounded up unless the user has Magic Guard or Rock Head , or the target has Sap Sipper ; its Speed is not lowered.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description LA The user launches its amassed chlorophyll to inflict damage on the target. This also damages the user and lowers the user's action speed. S V The user launches its amassed chlorophyll to inflict damage on the target. This also damages the user.

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] This move's name may be a pun on chloroplast , a chlorophyll-rich organelle in plant and algae cells responsible for photosynthesis. Chloroblast is tied with Frenzy Plant for the highest base power of all Grass-type attacks, excluding Z-Moves , Max Moves , and G-Max Moves . Chloroblast is the only move to have its recoil negated by Rock Head but not receive a boost from Reckless .
